violations.text: Kombucha and cheese in the front display cooler were observed held at 43 to 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items to be moved to colder refrigeration for proper cold holding temperatures. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

At the time of inspection, the front area display cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 43 degrees Fahrenheit with food/beverage temperatures observed at 43 to 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit is to be adjusted to properly maintain food temperatures at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

The opening behind the ovens and the passageway to the mop sink were observed to have exposed wood/structure which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for floors/walls/ceilings must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The floor in the dish area is constructed of wood flooring with a worn sealed coating which is no longer durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan cover in the walk-in cooler has an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
